etymology.description: Phonetic: 𡍬 (originally 𧸇, further reduced to 士(士) (shì, "scholar") and 四(四) (sì, "four")) + Semantic: 貝(貝) (bèi, "cowry") -> meaning 'to sell; trade'
etymology.interpretation: 𧶠 combines the phonetic component 𡍬, representing sound, with the semantic component 貝(貝) (bèi, "cowry"), symbolizing value or trade. The evolution to its current form involved a reduction to 士(士) and 四(四), which further altered its appearance.
